meals.name: Ultimate Spaghetti Carbonara
meals.description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, pecorino, parmesan, and pancetta. The key is working quickly off the heat so the eggs emulsify into a silky sauce rather than scrambling.
ingredients: 200g pancetta or guanciale, cut into small cubes
50g pecorino cheese, finely grated, plus extra to serve
2 plump garlic cloves, peeled and left whole
sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Bring a large pan of salted water to the boil.
Whisk the eggs with most of the pecorino and parmesan in a bowl. Season generously with black pepper and set aside.

Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add the pancetta and fry for 5–6 mins until golden and crisp. Add the whole garlic cloves and cook for 2 mins more, then discard the garlic.
Pour in the white wine and let it bubble for 1 min until mostly evaporated.
Cook the spaghetti according to pack instructions until al dente. Before draining, scoop out a mugful (about 200ml) of starchy pasta cooking water.
Drain the pasta and immediately tip it into the pan with the pancetta. Toss well over a low heat for 30 seconds, then remove the pan from the heat.

Quickly pour in the egg and cheese mixture, tossing constantly and adding splashes of the reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce into a silky, glossy coating. The residual heat should cook the eggs gently without scrambling them.
Divide between warmed bowls and serve immediately with extra grated cheese and black pepper.

sources.title: BBC Good Food — Ultimate Spaghetti Carbonara
